response:
THE ART GALLERY SHOWCASES DIGITAL INSTALLATIONS USING SOPHISTICATED ALGORITHMS.
THE FIRST INSTALLATION USES A PARAMETRIC EQUATION TO GENERATE DYNAMIC PATTERNS.
FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE RATIO A/B MUST BE RATIONAL.
LET A/B EQUAL P/Q WHERE P AND Q ARE CO-PRIME POSITIVE INTEGERS.
THE PHASE SHIFT PHI DOES NOT AFFECT WHETHER THE LOOP CLOSES BUT CHANGES ITS SHAPE.
THE PERIOD T OF THE MOTION IS EQUAL TO 2PI TIMES THE LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE OF A AND B.
THE SECOND INSTALLATION DISPLAYS A THREE DIMENSIONAL SURFACE DEFINED BY AN IMPLICIT FUNCTION.
THE SURFACE TOPOLOGY CHANGES WHEN THE PARAMETER LAMBDA REACHES CRITICAL VALUES OF ONE AND NEGATIVE TWO.
THE JOURNALIST FINDS THIS ANALYSIS GOOD FOR UNDERSTANDING THE VISUAL EVOLUTION OF THE ART.
AT LAMBDA EQUALS ONE THE SURFACE BECOMES A FLAT PLANE INTERSECTING THE SPHERE.
AT LAMBDA EQUALS NEGATIVE TWO THE SURFACE SPLIT INTO SEPARATE COMPONENTS OR PINCHES.
THESE MATHEMATICAL THRESHOLDS MARK THE TRANSITION BETWEEN DIFFERENT GEOMETRIC FORMS.
VISITORS CAN OBSERVE HOW THE SHAPE MORPHS AS THE PARAMETER VARIES ACROSS THE RANGE.
THE INTERSECTION OF ART AND TECHNOLOGY CREATES A FASCINATING EXPERIENCE FOR ALL.
THE EXHIBITION DEMONSTRATES HOW ALGORITHMS CAN GENERATE COMPLEX AND BEAUTIFUL DESIGNS.
